programmes.show_description: Glen covers Rachel's feline failure. Jay tackles Mark's Greek tragedy. Sketch knocks out Emma's punchy cover-up. Alice helps Lois celebrate her penguin persona.
Ardal O'Hanlon makes some sheep, Chris Ramsey gets younger, Judi Love becomes a queen, Sophie Duker wets teabags, and Bridget Christie gets lost in a small field.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Jon Richardson and Sara Pascoe take on Sean Lock and Richard Ayoade. Nick Helm joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
Teleshopping
Teleshopping
Courtland Gray, chief operating officer of Mississippi-based audio equipment manufacturer Peavey Electronics, goes incognito in his workforce. How do the front-line staff feel?
Todd Pedersen, the founder and CEO of Vivint, a leading solar and home security firm based in Utah, goes undercover among his employees, posing as a reality show contestant.
...Selling: Stuck in real estate limbo, Chandra and Tom have decreased the value of their property. The brothers attempt to raise the bar with an ambitious upgrade. Will it work?
In Gloucestershire, there's a malfunctioning blender, a singing dog, and plenty of catty comments, as Debbie and Paul, Angela and John, and Kirsty and James compete.
In Cheshire, bossy Faye and Chris play it safe with tried-and-tested family favourites, romantic Mark and Shaun serve a wholesome menu, and chatterbox Dawn and Keith plan ambitious dishes.
The Wacky West Country One: What will bride Alex, who loves Pride and Prejudice, make of pig racing and her groom, Mark, in dungarees and wellies on their big day?
The Bungee One: Will bride-to-be Ellie, who hates flying, go along with her groom Craig's heart-stopping ideas?
Ashley and Brandon Wright, the co-owners of Hamburger Mary's, go incognito in their company, to ensure that their culture of diversity is being maintained as the business grows.
Dan Duffy, CEO of the United Real Estate Group, goes undercover in his own firm. He meets different employees and learns about their professional and personal lives.
Cafe 36: Gordon Ramsay visits Cafe 36 in Chicago. Husband-and-wife team Terry and Carol are stuck in the past and their head chef's lax attitude is driving away customers.
Casa Roma: Gordon visits Casa Roma, the oldest restaurant in Lancaster, California. Although the bar is a success, the restaurant is failing and has had 20 chefs in two years.
...and Selling: Brand new series: Nurse Jenna and firefighter Neil must sell his frat house so they can live together. Can his place be revived and smoke out the competition?
Love Me Tender: Jared and Kyle clash. There's a difficult docking. Eileen gets under everyone's skin. And a night in the hot tub has repercussions.
Emotions run high as Bridget Christie shoves a cuddly toy inside a traffic cone. Sophie Duker does a strange dinosaur impression. Plus: robots, and an impressive hose!
Jimmy Carr hosts as Jon Richardson and Joe Wilkinson take on Lee Mack and Katherine Ryan. Brett Domino joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
Jimmy Carr hosts, as Sean Lock and Harry Hill take on Jon Richardson and Rosie Jones. Mr Swallow, aka Nick Mohammed, joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
Mark's Women: Mark gets into online gaming and live role play with Dobby and wonders whether she may really be 'the one'. Meanwhile, Jeremy gets involved with a cult.
Jeremy at JLB: Newly promoted Mark decides to enjoy his power and wealth by splashing out on a boiler and a 'creamy elephant' sofa, and by finding Jeremy a job in the office.
Alice covers Tara's childish crayon, Glen rids Alex of his hero-worshipping howler, Sketch helps tame Aaron's little monster and Jay suffers a case of beard envy.
Emotions run high as Bridget Christie shoves a cuddly toy inside a traffic cone. Sophie Duker does a strange dinosaur impression. Plus: robots, and an impressive hose!
Jimmy Carr hosts as Jon Richardson and Joe Wilkinson take on Lee Mack and Katherine Ryan. Brett Domino joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
Teleshopping
Teleshopping
Nader Masadeh, the president and CEO of Buffalo Wings & Rings, goes undercover in his own company to ensure that his standards are being kept across the brand.
Shawnon Bellah, chief operating officer of Nestlé Toll House Café by Chip, works undercover among her staff, learning about cookie decoration and the drive-thru experience.
...Selling: Overworked nurse Olivia and fireman Dan are desperate for a fresh start. Can the brothers prescribe the remedy needed to bring their current home back to life?
In Essex, there are deep-fried jam sandwiches and some attention-grabbing burlesque, as three couples compete to host the best dinner party.
Three couples put their hosting skills to the test on Jersey, in a week featuring wedding talk, a chinchilla dinner guest and cocktails from a fake cow...
The Midsummer Night's Wedding One: James longs for a traditional big day, so how will he react when groom Jack books a cast of actors to perform a Shakespeare classic?

programmes.show_name: Don't Tell the Bride: Let It Snow
programmes.show_description: Steve from Rotherham plans a wedding with an icy twist for his bride Emma, who wants an impressive, upmarket affair. But will his big ideas leave him snowed under?
Cindy Galardi Culpepper, the CEO and chairperson of Wienerschnitzel, the world's largest hot dog chain, goes incognito among her staff to look at the company's communication.
Lowell Hawthorne, the president and CEO of Caribbean food producer Golden Krust, goes undercover among the workforce in his own company, to determine the state of the business.
Gordon Returns: Gordon Ramsay revisits some of the restaurants he set out to save, including The Mixing Bowl, The Olde Stone Mill, Peter's and Campania. Have things improved?
Hot Potato Café: The Hot Potato Café in Philadelphia has never made a profit. When the chips are down, can Gordon help save the restaurant?
...and Selling: Brand new series: Sheryl and Frank's renovations may be the least of the brothers' problems. Instead, earning Frank's trust proves to be as tough as nails.
Cat's Out of the Bag: Jared's in hot water, Ben finds himself in a difficult position, a stew gets troubling news, and a shock decision rocks the boat.
Alex Horne is pelted with milk and gin, Bridget Christie has many questions about shoes, Judi Love is bewildered by bunting, and Ardal O'Hanlon looks a bit bereft.
Jimmy Carr hosts, as Sarah Millican and Nish Kumar take on Jon Richardson and Maisie Adam. David O'Doherty joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
Jimmy Carr hosts, as Roisin Conaty and Asim Chaudhry take on Jon Richardson and Josh Widdicombe. Bill Bailey joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
The Test: Jeremy meets a new love, Russian émigré Elena. Meanwhile, Mark makes a final play for Dobby, the object of his desires. But Sophie has some news...
Jeremy in Love: Jeremy realises that he's in love with Elena and decides to be less selfish to win her affection. But is it a good idea to tell her? Mark isn't so sure...
